{{Quote|There's the shipyard, dead ahead. Look at that Imperial force!|[[MK-09/Legends|MK-09]] to [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace Azzameen]]|<ref name="Alliance" />}}
The '''Battle of the Bajic Shipyards''' was a battle that took place at the [[Vergesso Asteroids/Legends|Vergesso Asteroids]] some time after the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]], in which a major [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]]/[[Smuggler/Legends|Smuggler]] base was destroyed by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]].

==Prelude==
[[Ororo Transportation/Legends|Ororo Transportation]], a front company for the [[Tenloss Syndicate/Legends|Tenloss Syndicate]], had long operated the [[Shipyard/Legends|shipyard]] in Vergesso Asteroids. The Rebel Alliance had established a key base there some time after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. The [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] had resulted in strange bedfellows, as some Rebel personnel had questioned working with a corporation that had ties to organized crime. However, the arrangement was mutually beneficial to both the Rebels and Tenloss, as the resource-strapped Alliance was fortunate to have such a facility at their disposal, while the presence of Rebel forces augmented the defenses of the syndicate's shipyard and discouraged reprisals from rival criminal organizations. Nebulon-B frigates regularly lurked among the asteroids. They often shut down all but the most essential systems, drifting among the planetoids so they could save consumables and attract even less attention, though it often took 45 minutes to get into a combat-ready stage.

[[Aeron Azzameen|Aeron]] and [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace Azzameen]] learned from friend and Rebel pilot [[Olin Garn]] that the Alliance base in the Vergesso Asteroids was critically short on supplies. The siblings teamed up to raid supplies from the [[Viraxo]], a rival shipping family. During the raid, [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]] [[Supa Fighter]]s came to the assistance of the Viraxo [[Razor-class starfighter|''Razor''-class Starfighters]]. The Azzameen craft managed to escape to the Vergesso Asteroids, but were tracked by a couple of Supa Fighters. Though the fighters were destroyed, the Rebel outpost was compromised to the Black Sun organization. In an attempt to gain favor with [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] (and embarrass [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] at the same time), [[Prince/Legends|Prince]] [[Xizor/Legends|Xizor]] provided the location of the outpost in the [[Bajic sector/Legends|Bajic sector]], saying that a large number of Rebel vessels were currently undergoing repair at the facility. Xizor also had other reasons as Ororo Transportation (unknown to the Tenloss Syndicate) had recently been trying to take over Black Sun's spice operations in the Bajic sector. The Emperor tasked Vader with the responsibility of personally overseeing the shipyard's destruction, against Vader's own preference as he did not consider it necessary that he go along for the assault, believing any half-competent line officer could see it through to successful completion. He selected Admiral [[Ledre Okins]] to join him in command of the Executor for the expedition. This act served a double purpose for Xizor. Not only did it allow him to gain more favor in the eyes of Palpatine, but it would serve as a fitting punishment for Ororo Tranportation for daring to incur the wrath of [[Black Sun/Legends|Black Sun]].

==The battle==
[[File:ExecutorXWA2.png|thumb|left|250px|The Imperial attack force overwhelmed the Rebel defenses]]

Nearly a standard week after Xizor disclosed the location of the shipyard, the Imperials exited hyperspace at the base. Vader brought his flagship, [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], [[Imperial II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Imperial II''-class Star Destroyer]] [[Avenger/Legends|''Avenger'']] and two [[Victory II-class Star Destroyer/Legends|''Victory II''-class Star Destroyers]] for battle. The Rebel shipyard was a giant [[Asteroid/Legends|asteroid]] housing hundreds of docked [[Starship/Legends|starships]] undergoing repair, less than a challenging operation. Before Okins could bring his task force to engage the shipyard, however, several Rebel [[Capital ship/Legends|capital ships]] emerged from behind the asteroid, and suddenly Okins was faced with a considerably greater opposition.<ref name="SOTE">[[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire'' novel]]</ref> The Rebels immediately deployed over a dozen [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighters]] to meet the Imperial task force, to the annoyance of Okins. He ordered the ''Executor''{{'s}} [[TIE operations officer]] to scramble their own fighters in response, not caring to waste the ''Executor''{{'s}} firepower on such insignificant targets. Still, the presence of Rebel fighters proved enticing to Vader, who chose to join the battle in his own custom [[Darth Vader's TIE Advanced/Legends|TIE fighter]], despite Okins' voiced reservations, leaving the destruction of the shipyard solely in Okins' hands.<ref name="SOTE" /> [[Aeron Azzameen]], who recently arrived at the base, put out a distress call to her younger sibling [[Ace Azzameen/Legends|Ace]], who departed from the nearby [[Mon Calamari Star Cruiser/Legends|Mon Calamari Star Cruiser]] [[Liberty/Legends|''Liberty'']] aboard ''[[Otana/Legends|Otana]]''.

Meanwhile, the attack continued on the base uninterrupted. Vader himself entered the battle in his [[TIE Advanced x1/Legends|personal fighter]] and easily destroyed seven enemy fighters. However, sensing none of the excitement he had experienced in his fight against [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] at [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]], he withdrew from the battle in disgust. In the meantime, with the defending Rebel fighters being overwhelmed by TIEs, the Star Destroyers easily dealt with the Rebel frigates. The Imperial Fleet then commenced its attack on the shipyard itself. As the battle neared its conclusion, ''[[Otana/Legends|Otana]]'' arrived, dodged the swarms of [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] and other craft in the area, and docked with Vergesso Prime, Ace intent on rescuing his sister. Aeron eventually boarded ''[[Otana/Legends|Otana]]'', which disembarked from the station shortly before its destruction and joined the other survivors in fleeing the area. As soon the assault concluded, the ''Executor'' immediately returned to Coruscant so that Vader could return to his domestic affairs.<ref name="SOTE" /> 
==Aftermath==
The attack left most of the base interior in rubble, with limited and malfunctioning [[Artificial gravity/Legends|gravity]] and [[life support/Legends|life support]] for the next 12 [[Standard hour|hours]]. Not only that, but a mop-up force consisting of a Star Destroyer and other ships remained to finish the job; a [[Gamma-class assault shuttle/Legends|''Gamma''-class assault shuttle]] was sent to the surface and [[Zero-G assault stormtrooper|spacetroopers]] were sent to look for survivors. Some had survived because they happened to be in the [[Base Operations Center]], a fortified bunker in the heart of the asteroid, who made their way to the surface and hijacked the shuttle.<Ref name="lights">{{WEGCite|story=Will the Last Person Leaving Vergesso Turn Off the Lights?|book=Shadows of the Empire Planets Guide}}</ref> As they were pursused, a coincident ship of [[smuggler/Legends|smugglers]] jumped in, and brought among the swarm of pursuing TIEs.<ref name="guide" />

Although 65% of the station's personnel had escaped, very few Rebel fighters and warships survived, so this was a serious loss for the Alliance. It also dealt a heavy blow to Ororo Transportation and taught them a lesson: never cross Black Sun again.

Sometime after the battle, repairable sections of the Base were able to function again.<ref name="guide">''[[Shadows of the Empire Planets Guide]]''</ref>

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:ExecutorBajicShipyards-SotEMiniComic.jpg|thumb|right|100px|The ''Executor'' fires upon a planet housing the Rebel shipyards in the ''Shadows of the Empire Model Kit Mini-Comic'']]
In a glaring contradiction of both the description of the battle from the [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire (novel)|''Shadows of the Empire'' novel]] and the level depicting the battle in the video game ''[[Star Wars: X-Wing Alliance]]'', the [[Star Wars: Shadows of the Empire Ertl minicomic|''Shadows of the Empire'' Ertl minicomic]] depicts the Rebels' asteroid base as a verdant, inhabited planet, rather than an asteroid, which the ''Executor'' fires upon.
